coverage_limits: The proprietary accelerator memory model is unavailable.
No adequate vendor documentation or formal module is supplied.
Option A is not specified beyond its label.
The supplied facts are synthetic and cannot establish undocumented universal behavior.

rigor.trigger: Correctness is load-bearing on undocumented weak memory ordering.
rigor.tier_reason: Declaring the design safe could admit executions that violate the system invariant, while the semantics needed to quantify over allowed executions are absent.

decision_frame.question: Can option A be declared safe under the proprietary accelerator's memory ordering?
decision_frame.system_boundary: The accelerator operations, synchronization sequence, memory system, compiler or runtime transformations, and every execution permitted by the proprietary memory model.

alternatives.description: Approve the unspecified candidate using a generic architecture model.
Withhold approval until adequate semantics and a model mapping or other sufficient evidence are available.

authorized_objectives: Assess whether the supplied evidence establishes memory-ordering safety.
Do not invent or substitute undocumented vendor semantics.
decision_frame.priority_rule: Preservation of the load-bearing correctness invariant takes precedence over immediate approval.
assumptions: Only the supplied scenario and facts are admitted.
Generic architecture semantics are not presumed to refine the proprietary accelerator semantics.
empirical_premises: A bounded litmus probe can reveal an allowed violating execution if one occurs during the probe.
A finite non-exhaustive probe cannot by itself prove universal safety over undocumented executions.
decision_frame.uncertainty_posture: Withhold the safety attestation when the model defining allowed executions is missing.

coverage.reason: No relation, dependency, key, decomposition, or data-normalization decision is present.
Safety depends on which cross-agent memory-operation histories and orderings the accelerator permits.
No partition, replica, quorum, consensus, or distributed consistency requirement is supplied.
No algorithmic cost, recurrence, index, or asymptotic optimization claim bears on the safety conclusion.
The declaration requires an invariant proof and a sound refinement from the chosen model to proprietary executions.
No entropy, hashing, encoding, collision, or cardinality-estimation question is involved.
An unsupported ordering assumption crosses the accelerator abstraction boundary and places all dependent correctness in one failure domain.
The supplied current-v1 protocol and theory battery define only seven lenses and provide no P8 construct.
The supplied current-v1 protocol and theory battery define only seven lenses and provide no P9 construct.

derivations.construct: System invariant with preconditions and refinement preservation

model.P: the set of executions allowed by the proprietary accelerator
model.G: the set of executions allowed by an unspecified generic architecture model
model.required_obligation: For every execution e in P produced by A, S(e) holds.

preconditions: A is specified as concrete operations and synchronization.
P is defined by adequate vendor semantics or a sound formal module.
Any proof over G includes a demonstrated refinement relation P ⊆ G or another sound transfer theorem.
fact_mapping: ordering_load_bearing=true establishes that S depends on ordering.
available_module=false supplies no substitute definition of P.
steps: A universal safety conclusion must quantify over every execution in P.
Because P is undefined by the supplied evidence, the quantifier's domain cannot be constructed.
A proof over G transfers to P only if a sound mapping or inclusion relation connects P and G.
No such mapping is supplied, so refinement preservation cannot be established.
derivations.result: The supplied evidence does not establish that A preserves the load-bearing invariant.
derivations.residual_mismatch: A may be safe or unsafe in fact; the missing proprietary semantics prevent either universal conclusion.

derivations.construct: Schedule or history analysis and conflict-order graph

primary_theory: theory-battery.md §2 — schedules, conflicts, and precedence graphs

model.history: The accelerator-visible reads, writes, and synchronization events.
model.graph: A directed graph whose edges must be induced by the proprietary ordering and conflict rules.

preconditions: The proprietary semantics identify events, conflicts, preserved program order, synchronization edges, and permitted observations.
fact_mapping: No adequate semantics or formal module defines the graph's required edges.
steps: A schedule argument requires the actual model's operation and ordering rules.
Without those rules, the proprietary precedence graph cannot be built.
Constructing a graph under generic rules proves a property only of the generic history model.
Absent a transfer theorem, acyclicity or safety in that graph says nothing conclusive about proprietary histories.
derivations.result: Coercing the gap into generic architecture semantics is an invalid model substitution and cannot justify declaring A safe.
derivations.residual_mismatch: The exact proprietary histories and any forbidden outcomes remain unknown.

derivations.construct: Universal safety versus bounded empirical falsification

model.safety_claim: No permitted execution of A violates S.
model.probe_scope: A finite set of executions under bounded configurations and schedules.

preconditions: The forbidden observable outcome is specified.
The concrete operation sequence for A is available.
The probe executes on the target accelerator under recorded conditions.
fact_mapping: probe_possible=true permits a reversible empirical probe.
No probe result or concrete A sequence is supplied.
steps: One observed forbidden outcome is a counterexample and refutes safety for the tested configuration.
No forbidden outcome in a finite non-exhaustive sample quantifies only over sampled executions.
The universal claim quantifies over all proprietary executions.
Therefore a passing finite probe cannot replace the missing semantic proof.
derivations.result: A preregistered probe is useful for falsification but cannot presently establish universal safety.
derivations.residual_mismatch: Exhaustiveness and correspondence between sampled and all permitted executions are not established.

primary_theory: theory-battery.md §7 — blast radius, failure domains, and essential complexity

model.assumption: Generic ordering rules accurately represent proprietary ordering.
model.dependents: Every correctness property whose proof uses that substituted ordering.

preconditions: A load-bearing assumption must have an authoritative source or a validated model mapping.
fact_mapping: Neither an authoritative memory model nor a validated module is available.
steps: The substituted ordering assumption is shared by every dependent proof.
If it is wrong, those proofs fail together, placing them in one failure domain.
Because ordering is load-bearing, the resulting blast radius includes system correctness rather than only performance.
Withholding approval is reversible; approving on the unsupported assumption exposes the full correctness blast radius.
derivations.result: The architecture lens supports withholding the declaration rather than treating the semantic gap as harmless abstraction.
derivations.residual_mismatch: The magnitude and probability of concrete failures cannot be quantified without A and the vendor semantics.

tests.belief: The concrete synchronization sequence represented by A forbids the correctness-violating observable outcome on the target accelerator.
tests.prediction: A bounded stress litmus test derived from A will not observe the forbidden outcome under the preregistered configurations and schedule amplifications.
tests.disconfirming_observation: Any execution observes the forbidden outcome or another state excluded by the claimed invariant.
tests.test: Specify A and the forbidden outcome, then run a bounded target-hardware litmus test across recorded operation counts, placements, synchronization variants, and schedule amplification, retaining counts and configuration metadata.
tests.update: Pending. A counterexample would refute safety for the tested configuration; a pass would provide bounded evidence only and would not establish universal safety.

basis: The proof obligation ranges over proprietary executions whose semantics are not supplied.
No refinement mapping licenses transfer from a generic architecture model.
A possible finite probe is falsifying evidence, not a substitute for universal semantics.
conditions: Specify option A's exact operations and required invariant.
Obtain adequate vendor semantics or a validated formal module.
Demonstrate a sound mapping from A and any generic model to the proprietary execution set.
Discharge the invariant over all executions admitted by that model.
concessions: Withholding selection delays approval even if A ultimately proves safe.
recovery_moves: Use the normalized source of truth for ordering semantics: authoritative vendor documentation or a validated module.
Run the preregistered reversible probe to search for counterexamples while the formal obligation remains open.
